title: Planet NASA Kepler name, if applicable.
Planet NASA Kepler Object of Interest (KOI) name, if applicable.
Planet habitable zone classification (hot, warm, or cold).
Planet Mass Class (mercurian, subterran, terran, superterran, neptunian, or jovian).
Planet Composition Class (iron, rocky-iron, rocky-water, water-gas, gas).
Planet Habitable Class (mesoplanet, thermoplanet, psychroplanet, hypopsychroplanet, hyperthermoplanet, non-habitable).
Planet mass. Most of the values were estimated from minimum mass.
Planet radius. Most of these values were estimated for confirmed planets.
Planet magnitude as seen from a Moon-Earth distance (Moon = -12.7).
Planet apparent size as seen from a Moon-Earth distance (Moon = 0.5°).
Planet mean distance from the star.
Planet inclination (assumed 60° when not available, and 90° for Kepler data).
Star name from the Henry Draper Star Catalog.
Star name from the Hipparchus Star Catalog.
Star apparent visual magnitude from Earth.
Star apparent visual magnitude from planet (Sun = -25 from Earth).
Star apparent size from planet (Sun = 0.5° from Earth).
Star number of planets in the habitable zone.
Star inner edge of habitable zone.
Star outer edge of habitable zone.
Star is in the HabCat database.
Planet is candidate for potential habitable exomoons.
Planet is confirmed (relevant to the Kepler data).
Planet discovery method (rv = radial velocity, tran = transiting, ima = imaging, micro = micro lensing, pul = pulsar timing, ast = astrometry).
